name: Spectran Inc
recent_news: No evidence of a school-bus operator named 'Spectran Inc' at 216 W 1st St, Wayne, NE. That address is the headquarters of Mid States School Bus, Inc., which was acquired by First Student (FirstGroup) in March 2021 (~200 buses, ~20 districts in eastern NE/SD). A separate Spectran Inc (USDOT 256480) exists in Elk Grove Village, IL as a freight carrier (~36 power units). The NE record appears to be a database/listing artifact, not an independent school-bus operator.
website: nebraskaschoolbus.com (resolves to Mid States School Bus; not a separate Spectran site)
fit_notes: Not a tuck-in target. Address ties to Mid States School Bus, which is already owned by First Student (a direct Summit competitor / EQT-backed platform). Spectran Inc itself is a freight carrier, not a school-bus operator. Skip.

name: Integrity Wheelchair
owner_or_parent: possible affiliation with A-TEC Ambulance, Inc.
principals: Dave Davis (Vice President per source data)
districts_or_customers: Crystal Lake / McHenry County, IL service area; multiple directory listings tie phone (815) 479-1615 to A-TEC Ambulance
recent_news: No M&A or growth news located. Listings link Integrity Wheelchair Transport Service to A-TEC Ambulance, Inc. of Crystal Lake/Elgin, IL via shared phone number (815) 479-1615 and atecambulance.com domain, suggesting it may be an A-TEC-affiliated wheelchair van division rather than a fully standalone firm.
fit_notes: Very small wheelchair/NEMT operator (~9 employees, ~$0.7M revenue) with no confirmed K-12 SPED contracts. Likely subscale and outside Summit's school-bus tuck-in focus; only relevant if Summit is pursuing IL SPED van rollups, otherwise pass.

name: Senior Independence Trans
owner_or_parent: Ohio Living (formerly Ohio Presbyterian Retirement Services / OPRS) — non-profit senior care system
service_types: non-emergency medical / community-based services for OPRS/Ohio Living residents
districts_or_customers: Ohio Living Breckenridge Village (Willoughby, OH) and other Ohio Living campuses
recent_news: Senior Independence was OPRS's home- and community-based services brand. In October 2016 OPRS rebranded to Ohio Living and Senior Independence became Ohio Living Home Health & Hospice. The Willoughby listing is a residual local-business listing tied to the senior-care system, not an independent for-profit transport operator.
website: none (organization rolled into ohioliving.org / Ohio Living Home Health & Hospice)
fit_notes: Not a tuck-in target. Part of a non-profit senior-living system (Ohio Living), serves residents not K-12 students, no school-bus or SPED footprint. Skip.

name: Lexi Medical Transport LLC
districts_or_customers: Manatee and Sarasota County, FL (NEMT, not school district)
recent_news: NPI 1437631249 lists it as a Non-emergency Medical Transport (VAN) provider in Bradenton, FL. No M&A or growth news; small founder-operated business with strong (5-star, ~6 reviews) but limited online footprint. No school district contracts identified.
website: leximedicaltransportllc.com (listed; domain unreachable on fetch attempt)
fit_notes: Independent micro-NEMT (~4 employees, ~$0.26M revenue), founded 2018, sole proprietor (Elexis Richardson). Not a school-bus operator and well below Summit's tuck-in size threshold. Pass unless Summit pursues FL SPED-van rollups.

name: Old School Transportation LLC
service_types: likely freight/truckload (not school bus) based on web footprint at oldschooltrans.com
recent_news: State field 'LA' conflicts with the listed Mesa, AZ address. The closest matching web entity is 'Old School Transportation Management' (oldschooltrans.com) — a truckload / flatbed / freight brokerage operating as a West Motor Freight / Evans Delivery agency, not a school-bus contractor. Multiple unrelated 'Old School Transport/ation' DOT filings exist (IA, IL inactive, PA) but none are confirmed school-bus operators. No evidence of school-district contracts or pupil transport service.
website: oldschooltrans.com (freight; not confirmed for this LLC) — none verified for the Mesa AZ entity
fit_notes: Despite the 'school' in the name and the school=1 flag, the only web evidence points to freight/truckload operations, not pupil transportation. The LA state vs. Mesa AZ address mismatch further suggests a data-quality issue. Likely NOT a school-bus tuck-in target; deprioritize pending direct confirmation.
